title: 埃及十字架之谜
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\27-埃及十字架之谜-埃勒里奎因.txt
summary:
In Thomas Braid's study, Vawn informs Ellery Queen that a red pawn was discovered near a totem pole. Ellery examines a round reading table in the room and compares tobacco ash from a pipe found in the gazebo to tobacco in a cigar box belonging to Braid. During his search, Ellery discovers a hidden drawer containing a collection of high-quality pipes made of materials such as sepiolite and celluloid. Vawn presents a unique Poseidon trident pipe, which Ellery suggests was likely a gift due to its unusual design.

Ellery then examines a custom, wall-embedded foldable chess table featuring a mother-of-pearl edge. While the board contains several black and red pieces, Ellery notes that only eleven red pieces are present, implying the twelfth is the piece found by the totem pole. Questioning why a wealthy enthusiast would use cheap wooden pieces for such an expensive table, Ellery is answered by the butler, Stollings. Stollings explains that Braid's expensive ivory set was being copied by a carver in Brooklyn as a surprise for Mr. Temple, forcing Braid to use temporary wooden pieces.

Stollings recounts the events of the night of the incident, noting that Braid instructed Stollings, Fox, and Mrs. Baxter to go to bed early so he could be alone. Stollings observed that Braid appeared tense and was playing chess against himself. Braid initially sat in one chair but moved to another after making a move with a red piece. Stollings and the others subsequently left in a car driven by Fox, who dropped them at the Roxy Theater.
